description: Persist learning pairs that curate dedup found distinct so they are skipped on subsequent runs

criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy` output
Rust: All tests pass with `cargo test`
No breaking changes to existing APIs unless explicitly required
All dismissal pairs normalized: id_lo < id_hi
Dismissals never recorded during dry_run or on LLM error batches
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

priorityPhilosophy.description: Hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN — Anticipate edge cases before coding
2. PHASE 2 FOUNDATION — ~1 day now to save ~2+ weeks later (1:10+ ratio); we are pre-launch, foundations compound
3. FUNCTIONING CODE — Pragmatic, reliable, wired in per plan
4. CORRECTNESS — Compiles, type-checks, scoped tests pass deterministically
5. CODE QUALITY — Clean code, qualityDimensions satisfied, no warnings
principles: Quality dimensions explicit — qualityDimensions on every task tells you what 'good' looks like
Edge cases = test cases — every known edge case must have a corresponding test
Scoped per-iteration tests, full suite at REVIEW-001 — REVIEW must leave the trunk green including pre-existing failures
Ship working code with tests to prove it; handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production

prohibitedOutcomes: Tests that only assert 'no crash' or check type without verifying content
Tests that mirror implementation internals (break when refactoring)
Abstractions with only one concrete use
Error messages that don't identify what went wrong
Catch-all error handlers that swallow context
INSERT OR IGNORE that silently drops errors other than UNIQUE constraint
Recording dismissals inside dry-run mode (violates read-only convention)
Recording dismissals when the LLM call errored (can't trust partial results)

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: v17 migration creates dedup_dismissals table with (id_lo, id_hi) PK and idx_dedup_dismissals_hi index
Positive: v17 down migration drops the table and index, reverts schema_version to 16
Positive: CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION is 17 after migration
Unit test: load_dismissals on empty table returns empty HashSet
Unit test: record_dismissals([5,3], [10,7]) stores as (3,5) and (7,10) — normalization verified
Unit test: record_dismissals called twice with same pairs does not error (INSERT OR IGNORE)
Unit test: is_fully_dismissed([1,2,3], {(1,2),(1,3),(2,3)}) returns true
Unit test: is_fully_dismissed([1,2,3], {(1,2),(1,3)}) returns false — missing (2,3)
Unit test: is_fully_dismissed([1], dismissals) returns false — single item
Unit test: clear_dismissals removes all rows, subsequent load returns empty
Known-bad: is_fully_dismissed must check ALL C(N,2) pairs, not just adjacent pairs — test with 4+ IDs where only some pairs exist
Negative: No unwrap() in any helper function
Negative: record_dismissals with empty slice must not execute any SQL
userStories.description:
Create the v17 migration adding the `dedup_dismissals` table and implement the four CRUD helper functions that the rest of the feature depends on.

DO:
- Create `src/db/migrations/v17.rs` with CREATE TABLE + index, following v16.rs pattern exactly
- Register v17 in `src/db/migrations/mod.rs` (mod, MIGRATIONS array, CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION = 17)
- Implement four functions in `src/commands/curate/mod.rs`:
  1. `load_dismissals(conn) -> HashSet<(i64, i64)>` — SELECT all pairs
  2. `record_dismissals(conn, pairs: &[(i64, i64)])` — multi-row INSERT OR IGNORE, normalize id_lo < id_hi
  3. `clear_dismissals(conn) -> TaskMgrResult<usize>` — DELETE FROM, return rows deleted
  4. `is_fully_dismissed(cluster_ids: &[i64], dismissals: &HashSet<(i64, i64)>) -> bool` — pure function, checks all C(N,2) pairs
- Write migration tests in v17.rs (table exists, down removes it, version = 17)
- Write unit tests for all four helpers in `src/commands/curate/tests.rs`

DO NOT:
- Add foreign keys to dedup_dismissals (dismissed pairs for retired learnings are inert)
- Use unwrap() in production code
- Store pairs without normalization (always id_lo < id_hi)
- Use per-row INSERT — batch into multi-row INSERT OR IGNORE

Edge cases:
- Empty pairs slice passed to record_dismissals → no-op, no SQL executed
- Duplicate pairs in input → INSERT OR IGNORE handles naturally
- Single-item cluster → is_fully_dismissed returns false (< 2 members)
- Two-item cluster → checks exactly 1 pair

Known-bad to guard against:
- Recording (5, 3) instead of (3, 5) — normalization must swap
- is_fully_dismissed returning true for a cluster where SOME but not ALL pairs are dismissed
edgeCases: Empty pairs slice: record_dismissals returns immediately, no SQL
Reversed pair (5, 3): normalized to (3, 5) before insert
Duplicate pairs in single call: INSERT OR IGNORE deduplicates
Two-item cluster: checks exactly one pair
Large cluster (7 items): checks all 21 pairs

failureModes.cause: record_dismissals called with thousands of pairs
failureModes.expectedBehavior: SQLite handles multi-row INSERT fine at this scale (~300 rows max in practice)

userStories.notes:
Follow v16.rs migration pattern exactly (static Migration, up/down SQL, inline tests). Use `conn.unchecked_transaction()` for the batch INSERT. Use `rusqlite::params_from_iter()` for dynamic VALUES clause. Test helpers: setup_db() at tests.rs:30, insert_learning_full() at tests.rs:1830.

Learning [22]: New migration columns default to NULL for backward compatibility.
Learning [1646]: Database migration module structure and registration pattern.
Learning [1393]: Migration version tests should use >= not == for forward compatibility.
Learning [833]: Don't add migration columns to base schema CREATE TABLE definitions.
Learning [348]: Down migrations pattern — for tables (vs columns), use DROP TABLE IF EXISTS.
qualityDimensions: Pair normalization is enforced at write time (id_lo < id_hi invariant)
Multi-row INSERT for efficiency — never per-row inserts
is_fully_dismissed is a pure function with no DB access
Empty-input edge cases handled without executing SQL
userStories.title: v17 migration + dismissal CRUD helpers

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: DedupParams has reset_dismissals field, DedupResult has clusters_skipped field
Positive: --reset-dismissals flag appears in CLI help for curate dedup
Positive: When all clusters fully dismissed, curate_dedup returns clusters_skipped > 0 and makes zero LLM calls
Positive: When LLM returns [] for a batch of N items, all C(N,2) pairs recorded as dismissed
Positive: When LLM returns a cluster {A,B} from batch {A,B,C,D}, pairs (A,C),(A,D),(B,C),(B,D),(C,D) are dismissed but (A,B) is NOT
Positive: format_dedup_text shows 'N cluster(s) skipped' when clusters_skipped > 0
Positive: stderr shows 'Skipped N cluster(s) (all pairs previously dismissed)' when applicable
Negative: dry_run=true must NOT record any dismissals (dedup_dismissals table empty after dry run)
Negative: Batch with LLM error (raw_clusters: Err(())) must NOT record dismissals
Negative: Pairs involving IDs in merged_ids must NOT be recorded as dismissed
CONTRACT: batch_item_ids indexed by batch_idx matches the batches consumed by process_batches_parallel
Known-bad: If dismissal recording happens inside process_batches_parallel worker threads, it would require passing conn across thread boundary — must happen in main thread post-processing loop
userStories.description:
Integrate the CRUD helpers from FEAT-001 into the `curate_dedup()` orchestrator so that:
1. Previously-dismissed clusters are skipped before LLM calls
2. Non-duplicate pairs from LLM results are recorded as dismissed
3. The --reset-dismissals flag clears the table before running

DO:
- Add `reset_dismissals: bool` to `DedupParams` in types.rs (default false)
- Add `clusters_skipped: usize` to `DedupResult` in types.rs (after clusters_found)
- Add `--reset-dismissals` flag to `CurateAction::Dedup` in cli/commands.rs
- Pass `reset_dismissals` through in main.rs CurateAction::Dedup handler
- In curate_dedup():
  a. If reset_dismissals, call clear_dismissals(conn) early
  b. Load dismissals via load_dismissals(conn)
  c. In embedding pre-filter path: skip clusters where is_fully_dismissed returns true
  d. In standard batch path: same check per batch
  e. Save batch_item_ids: Vec<Vec<i64>> BEFORE calling process_batches_parallel
  f. After processing each batch output: compute dismissed pairs (all batch pairs minus merged-cluster pairs minus merged_ids) and call record_dismissals
  g. Skip recording when dry_run=true or batch had LLM error
- Update format_dedup_text() in output.rs to show clusters_skipped count
- Add eprintln! for skipped clusters matching existing stderr pattern

DO NOT:
- Record dismissals during dry_run (read-only convention)
- Record dismissals when batch had LLM error (can't trust results)
- Record dismissal pairs involving IDs already in merged_ids (they're retired)
- Modify process_batches_parallel() — recording happens in main-thread post-processing loop
- Remove or modify existing merge/retire logic

Edge cases:
- All clusters in a run are dismissed → 0 batches sent to LLM, clusters_skipped = N
- Mixed: some clusters dismissed, some not → only non-dismissed sent to LLM
- Batch with partial merges: IDs in clusters form merged pairs; remaining IDs form dismissed pairs
- reset_dismissals + dry_run: table cleared but no new dismissals recorded

Known-bad to guard against:
- Recording dismissals for IDs that were merged in a prior batch (they're in merged_ids and retired)
- Forgetting to save batch_item_ids before process_batches_parallel consumes the batches Vec
- Recording cross-cluster pairs as dismissed when they should be (clusters in same batch ARE distinct from each other)
edgeCases: All clusters dismissed: zero batches sent to LLM, clusters_skipped reflects total
No dismissals exist (first run): all clusters sent to LLM as before
reset_dismissals + dry_run: clears table, runs read-only, no new dismissals
Batch where every learning ends up in one big cluster: no dismissed pairs (all merged)
Batch with LLM error: no dismissals recorded, llm_errors incremented as before

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Error logged to stderr, dedup continues processing remaining batches (best-effort)

failureModes.cause: load_dismissals fails (e.g., pre-v17 DB without table)
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Log warning, proceed with empty dismissals set (graceful degradation)

userStories.notes:
Key architectural constraint: process_batches_parallel workers don't access the DB (line 560 comment). All dismissal recording must happen in the main-thread loop at lines 885-964.

Batch ID tracking: save `let batch_item_ids: Vec<Vec<i64>> = batches.iter().map(|b| b.iter().map(|i| i.id).collect()).collect();` BEFORE calling process_batches_parallel (which consumes `batches`).

Dismissal pair computation per batch: given batch_ids and merged_cluster_ids, the dismissed pairs are all (i,j) pairs where i,j are in batch_ids, i != j, i and j are NOT both in the same cluster, and neither i nor j is in merged_ids.

Learning [1414]: Embedding pre-filter before LLM dedup reduces API calls.
Learning [488]: Skip extraction when SRP benefit is marginal — keep the filtering/recording logic inline in curate_dedup rather than extracting to separate functions.
Learning [1548]: Transaction safety — verify flush() is never called inside active tx.
qualityDimensions: Filtering happens before LLM calls — no wasted API usage on fully-dismissed clusters
Recording logic correctly excludes merged pairs and merged_ids
dry_run is strictly read-only (no side effects)
Batch item ID tracking preserves correct index alignment with BatchOutput
Output format matches existing stderr eprintln! patterns
userStories.title: Wire dismissal filtering and recording into curate_dedup

acceptanceCriteria: No code duplication (DRY principle)
Functions under 30 lines (flag complex ones)
Clear separation of concerns
Code follows existing project patterns
Dismissal helper functions are cohesive and well-placed
Any issues found spawn REFACTOR-FIX-xxx tasks via `task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001`
userStories.description:
Review all implementation for DRY violations, complexity, coupling, and maintainability.

DO: Check for duplicated logic, functions >30 lines, tight coupling between modules.
DO NOT: Skip — this gate catches issues before the final review.
userStories.notes: If issues found: `echo '{...}' | task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001` for each (priority 50-97). If no issues, invoke /simplify on any ugly touchpoint, then emit `<task-status>REFACTOR-001:done</task-status>`.
userStories.title: Review for refactoring opportunities

userStories.description:
Review all implementation for quality, security, integration wiring, and documentation needs. RUN THE FULL QUALITY GATE (unscoped test suite).

DO: Check every new function for error handling, check callers still work, verify no dead code, check if docs need updating. Run full cargo test / cargo clippy (no scope filter). Verify CLAUDE.md is updated with dedup_dismissals documentation.
DO NOT: Skip the integration wiring check — unconnected code is the #1 failure mode. Do NOT scope the test suite.
userStories.notes: Spawn fixes via `echo '{...}' | task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001`. If no issues: emit `<task-status>REVIEW-001:done</task-status>` with 'Clean review' note.
qualityDimensions: All new code wired to production entry point
userStories.title: Code review + final verification
